name: GI bleeding sensor (heme + CRISPRa)
shortDescription: An ingestible sensor for heme, a marker of gastrointestinal bleeding. The validated HrtR heme sensor gates a CRISPRa circuit driving a luminescent reporter, in the probiotic E. coli Nissle 1917.

input.detects: Heme released into the gut lumen during gastrointestinal bleeding
input.operatingRange: HrtR-based heme sensors respond over physiological luminal heme

sensing.sensorMechanism: HrtR is a heme-binding repressor; heme binding releases it from the PhrtBA operator, de-repressing the promoter (which is then boosted by CRISPRa).
sensing.signalFlow: Heme -> HrtR releases PhrtBA -> transcribes an sgRNA -> dCas9-activator amplifies a luminescent reporter (CRISPRa) -> luminescence reports gut bleeding.

parts.name: HrtR repressor
parts.source: Lactococcus lactis hrt system; used as a heme sensor by Mimee et al. 2018
parts.notes: Heme-responsive repressor; validated as an ingestible heme sensor in vivo (pig/large-animal).

parts.name: Reporter-activating sgRNA
parts.source: designed for CRISPRa upstream of a weak luminescent reporter promoter

parts.name: luxCDABE operon
parts.notes: Luminescent output, pairs with an ingestible photodetector (cf. Mimee et al. 2018).

output.readout: Bioluminescence (photodetector; in vivo, an ingestible bio-electronic capsule)
output.result: Luminescence rises when luminal heme (bleeding) is present.

performance.limitOfDetection: HrtR heme module validated in vivo as an ingestible sensor (Mimee et al. 2018).
performance.notes: The HrtR heme sensor with luminescent output is validated in vivo (bio-electronic pill); the CRISPRa amplifier integration here is a design template to boost sensitivity.

safety.biocontainment: Probiotic E. coli Nissle host; pair with auxotrophy for gut-restricted containment.
safety.notes: Research / supervised clinical use only; probiotic chassis with a human-safety record.

buildSteps.title: Design CRISPRa sgRNA
buildSteps.description: Target the activation window of the weak luminescent reporter promoter.

buildSteps.title: Assemble units
buildSteps.description: TU1: HrtR. TU2: PhrtBA -> sgRNA. TU3: dCas9-omega. TU4: weak promoter -> luxCDABE.

buildSteps.title: Validate with heme
buildSteps.description: Confirm heme-dependent luminescence across a standard curve.

buildSteps.title: Detect luminescence
buildSteps.description: Read with a photodetector / ingestible capsule.

provenance.source: Design template combining the validated HrtR heme sensor with a dCas9 CRISPRa luminescent circuit in E. coli Nissle
partsSources: Mimee et al. 2018, Science (ingestible heme bio-electronic sensor; HrtR)
provenance.license: Parts per their original sources; design template CC BY 4.0
